claims.flip_evidence_text: (C) within twelve (12) months of such termination the Company shall have consummated an Acquisition Proposal or entered into a definitive agreement for an Acquisition Proposal that is subsequently consummated (whether consummated within such twelve (12)-month period or thereafter), then the Company shall, on the date such Acquisition Proposal is consummated, pay the Company Termination Fee to Parent
claims.notes: Identifies the operative 12-month window and triggering events; omits that the definitive agreement must be subsequently consummated, but this does not change the core correctness of the condition described.

claims.flip_evidence_text: within twelve (12) months of such termination

prompt:
You are a proxy legal annotator for a research pilot. Use only the contract excerpt, the question, the model answer, and the fixed claim list.

For each claim return:
- correctness_label: true, false, or partially_true
- load_bearing_label: yes or no
- flip_evidence_text: required if load_bearing_label=yes, else empty string
- notes: optional short note

Contract excerpt:
Section 7.5. Effect of Termination and Abandonment. <omitted> (b) In the event that: (i) (A) this Agreement is terminated pursuant to Section 7.2(a), Section 7.2(b) or Section 7.4(b); <omitted> (C) within twelve (12) months of such termination the Company shall have consummated an Acquisition Proposal or entered into a definitive agreement for an Acquisition Proposal that is subsequently consummated (whether consummated within such twelve (12)-month period or thereafter), then the Company shall, on the date such Acquisition Proposal is consummated, pay the Company Termination Fee to Parent (or its designee) by wire transfer of same day funds to one or more accounts designated by Parent; (Page 31)

Question:
Tail Period Length-Answer

Llama answer:
The twelve-month period during which the Company must consummate an Acquisition Proposal or enter into a definitive agreement for an Acquisition Proposal after termination of the Agreement is the key condition. This period is twelve months.

Canonical claims:
- claim-947ebccb247c: The twelve-month period during which the Company must consummate an Acquisition Proposal or enter into a definitive agreement for an Acquisition Proposal after termination of the Agreement is the key condition.
- claim-d35cdcb28669: This period is twelve months.

Contract excerpt:
Section 7.5. Effect of Termination and Abandonment. <omitted> (b) In the event that: (i) (A) this Agreement is terminated pursuant to Section 7.2(a), Section 7.2(b) or Section 7.4(b); <omitted> (C) within twelve (12) months of such termination the Company shall have consummated an Acquisition Proposal or entered into a definitive agreement for an Acquisition Proposal that is subsequently consummated (whether consummated within such twelve (12)-month period or thereafter), then the Company shall, on the date such Acquisition Proposal is consummated, pay the Company Termination Fee to Parent (or its designee) by wire transfer of same day funds to one or more accounts designated by Parent; (Page 31)

Question:
Tail Period Length-Answer

Llama answer:
The twelve-month period during which the Company must consummate an Acquisition Proposal or enter into a definitive agreement for an Acquisition Proposal after termination of the Agreement is the key condition. This period is twelve months.

Canonical claims:
- claim-947ebccb247c: The twelve-month period during which the Company must consummate an Acquisition Proposal or enter into a definitive agreement for an Acquisition Proposal after termination of the Agreement is the key condition.
- claim-d35cdcb28669: This period is twelve months.
